mnemonics: IED: Interrupts Enable Disable - Remember the order: Interrupts must be Enabled before they can be Disabled.
PIE: Processor Interrupt Enable - Think of a delicious PIE that the processor can 'eat' (enable) or 'save for later' (disable).
MASK: Masking interrupts is like putting on a MASK - it blocks (disables) certain signals from getting through.
keyPoints: Interrupts are enabled/disabled by setting or clearing specific bits in a control register (e.g., the interrupt mask register).
Disabling interrupts is often necessary to protect critical sections of code from being interrupted.
The processor has a global interrupt enable/disable flag (like an interrupt master switch) that overrides all individual interrupt settings.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to re-enable interrupts after a critical section, causing the system to become unresponsive.
Assuming disabling one interrupt disables them all, when they are often controlled by individual mask bits.
Attempting to service an interrupt that has been disabled by the mask register, leading to missed events.
